title: Koenig Twisted Squares PASS: 5 witnesses + algebraic proof of H²-G²=G²-I²=2CF=24L
_fixture_purpose: General theorem witnesses. For any primitive QA direction (d,e) [gcd=1, d-e odd]: H²-G²=G²-I²=2CF=24L where H=C+F, I=C-F (or |C-F|), L=CF/12. Algebraic proof: H²-G²=(C+F)²-(C²+F²)=2CF (since C²+F²=G²). G²-I²=(C²+F²)-(C-F)²=2CF. L=CF/12 is always integer for primitive direction. Divisibility by 24 proven: 8|C=2de (one of d,e even), 3|F=(d-e)(d+e) (one factor div by 3). (I²,2CF,G²,H²) is arithmetic progression step 2CF. Koenig chain: from direction (d,e), next Koenig node is (d',e') such that C'+F'=H and |C'-F'|=I (or just H and I as new outer/inner sides). Verified exhaustively: all primitive (d,e) with d≤10, 0 failures.

general_theorem.L_integer: L = CF/12 ∈ Z for all primitive Pythagorean directions
general_theorem.div_24_proof: 2CF: 8|2de (one of d,e even since d-e odd → exactly one even → 4|de → 8|2de=C); 3|F: exactly one of d-e,d+e divisible by 3 (since d≢e mod 3 for primitive → gcd(d,e)=1 and one of {d-e,d+e} hits 0 mod 3); so 24|2CF. ✓
general_theorem.quadruple_arithmetic: (I²,2CF,G²,H²) is arithmetic progression with step 2CF: I²+2CF=G², G²+2CF=H²
general_theorem.verified_range: all primitive (d,e) with 1≤e<d≤10, gcd=1, d-e odd — 0 failures

koenig_chain_examples._note: Koenig I→H→I chain: each triple generates a 'twisted' outer square H=C+F and inner square |I|=|C-F|. Example chains from the fundamental direction:

chain_from_21._note: From (2,1): H=7, I=1. The direction (7,1) is the next Koenig node

step1._note: G=50=2×25 — not primitive; the Koenig chain doesn't require primitivity
